scenario: Tessera Bank runs Camunda 8.8 self-managed and 6 developers want an interactive Swagger UI for the Operate API to explore endpoints locally before writing curl scripts. The team needs the documented URL of the Swagger UI exposed inside a running Operate instance, including the self-managed default served on the regular HTTP port at localhost:8080.
question: Where is the Swagger UI exposed for an Operate API instance in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Swagger UI is available inside a running Operate at https://${base-url}/swagger-ui/index.html, and on self-managed installations the default URL is http://localhost:8080/swagger-ui.html
Swagger UI is hosted externally only at swagger.camunda.io with the cluster ID embedded as a query parameter, and it is not exposed inside the running Operate instance itself
Swagger UI is available inside a running Operate but only on the dedicated monitoring port 9600 of the broker, and it is not reachable on the regular HTTP port for any self-managed setup

Swagger UI is shipped only as a downloadable Postman collection that developers must import locally, while Operate itself does not bundle its own interactive Swagger interface in self-managed installations

a.text: Correct. The API Explorer section states a Swagger UI is available within a running instance of Operate at https://${base-url}/swagger-ui/index.html, with localhost:8080/swagger-ui.html as the self-managed default and a SaaS variant under https://${REGION}.operate.camunda.io/${CLUSTER_ID}/swagger-ui.html.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs point developers to a Swagger UI bundled inside the running Operate instance, not to a separate externally hosted Swagger site at swagger.camunda.io.

c.text: Incorrect. The self-managed Swagger URL uses port 8080, the regular Operate HTTP port, not the monitoring port 9600.

d.text: Incorrect. A Postman collection is mentioned as an additional way to work with the API, but Operate does bundle an interactive Swagger UI inside the running instance.

explanation: Operate exposes a Swagger UI from within each running instance at https://${base-url}/swagger-ui/index.html. On a typical self-managed install it lives at http://localhost:8080/swagger-ui.html, and SaaS uses https://${REGION}.operate.camunda.io/${CLUSTER_ID}/swagger-ui.html, all under the regular Operate HTTP port.
